sections.paragraph: f. i.q. נִשְׁכָּה (where see as to its origin [In Thes. suggested to be from לָשַׁךְ]), pl. לְשָׁכֹות constr. לִשְׁכֹות, a chamber, a cell, especially used of the cells of the temple, 1Ch 9:26 Eze 40:17, 45 Eze 40:45 42:1 , seq.; Neh 10:38, seq. Once, 1Sa 9:22, used of a dining rom; and Jer 36:12, of the room of the royal scribe in the royal palace. [See Thes.]
